segments.text: Confession.
I'm the coworker that dropped the bouncy ball bomb.
Sorry for the weird title, it's the best I could come up with to describe the chaos I unleashed at work.
Also, it's a throwaway account for obvious reasons.
I'm still dealing with the guilt, but here's hoping this confession somehow innocently entertains you during these tough times.
Some quick context.
I work in a large office building with around 300 employees spread across 10 floors.

Last week, the office management decided that work-life balance is important and decided to set up a relaxation zone on each floor, complete with lounge chairs, bean bags, and fun games like foosball, table tennis, and the root cause of my story, a bouncy ball pit.
One fateful day, I found myself alone in the relaxation zone during the lunch hour.
I started idly picking up a bouncy ball and throwing it into the pit.
It was a beautiful sight, multicolored bouncy balls jumping around happily.
A wicked thought crossed my mind.
Wouldn't it be fun to see them bounce around outside the pit?
I looked around to make sure no one was nearby, and following the impulse, tipped the pit slightly, letting loose around a hundred jubilantly colored balls on the entire floor.
What I did not anticipate was how bouncy these balls actually were.
With every bounce, they scattered in all directions, picking up a pattern that was unpredictable and chaotic.
To make matters worse, at that exact moment, my boss and his high value client chose to visit our floor.
To put it mildly, it was like a scene straight out of a slapstick comedy movie.
Imagine a high-profile client meeting being sabotaged by a sea of bouncy balls, causing people to slip, slalom, and skid.
To say it was chaotic would be an understatement.
I hid behind one of the large office plants, mortified and guilty for turning our professional workspace into a bad comedy sketch.
It was a mess.
Everyone thought it was an elaborate prank gone wrong.
I never confessed, of course, and the whole incident is under investigation.
Just to add to the drama, as I am typing this, I've heard from my workmate that there's talk about involving the security team to check the footage.
Since that happened, I'm terrified, feeling like a criminal for a crime I unintentionally committed.
So here I am, living with the guilt and the scare that I might have deeply tarnished my career over a harmless thought.
Now I'm debating if I should come clean or wait for them to possibly get to me.
Or maybe skim through the employee handbook to see if unleashing bouncy balls falls into grounds for employment termination.
We'll keep you updated, Reddit.
